code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    from collections import defaultdict
    import re

    # Helper to strip quotes and surrounding whitespace
    def clean(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Helper to strip unit parentheses like "(metres)" from numeric strings
    def strip_units(val: str) -> str:
        return re.sub(r"\s*\(.*?\)", "", val).strip()

    # Template functions for each predicate
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an architectural style of {clean(o)}",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"Construction of {s} began in {clean(o)}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed in {clean(o)}",
        "floorCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} floors",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {clean(o)}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"The cost of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "floorArea": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers a floor area of {clean(o)}",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"{s} was designed by {clean(o)}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {clean(o)}",
        "formerName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was formerly known as {clean(o)}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"The height of {s} is {strip_units(clean(o))} metres",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {clean(o)}",
        "developer": lambda s, o: f"{s} was developed by {clean(o)}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a tenant {clean(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {clean(o)}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {clean(o)}",
        "currentTenants": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently houses {clean(o)}",
        "address": lambda s, o: f"The address of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was inaugurated on {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the birthplace of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the death place of {s}",
        "bedCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} beds",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the region of {clean(o)}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {clean(o)}",
        "website": lambda s, o: f"The website of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"{s} was constructed in {clean(o)}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has NRHP reference number {clean(o)}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"{s} was added to the NRHP on {clean(o)}",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known for the building {clean(o)}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"The governing body of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the leader of {s}",
        "chancellor": lambda s, o: f"The chancellor of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a government type of {clean(o)}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the language of {s}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} {clean(s)} leads {s}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The main ethnic group in {s} is {clean(o)}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the hometown of {s}",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known for the project {clean(o)}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {clean(o)}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{s} has demonym {clean(o)}",
        "numberOfRooms": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} rooms",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {clean(o)}",
        "architecture": lambda s, o: f"{s} features architecture {clean(o)}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"The postal code of {s} is {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []

        # Process each triple using the appropriate template
        for tr in group:
            tmpl = templates.get(tr.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                parts.append(tmpl(subject, tr.object))
            else:
                # Fallback for unknown predicates
                parts.append(f"{subject} has {clean(tr.object)}")

        # Combine parts into a sentence
        sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all sentences
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
